REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT MAVERICK RESTAURANT GROUP, LLC'S A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. 1.AS TO DATTANI: IN THIS CONSOLIDATED ACTION THE CLAIMS BY DATTANI AGAINST THE MOVING PARTY INCLUDE CLAIMS MADE IN CASE 537353 AND 543635. THE NOTICE OF MOTION AND THE SEPARATE STATEMENT INDICATES THAT THIS MOTION IS ONLY MADE AS TO 543635, AND THEREFORE DOES NOT DISPOSE OF ALL THE CLAIMS IN THIS CONSOLIDATED ACTION. (SEE CCP 437C(A)). TO THE EXTENT THE MOVING PARTY PURPORTS THAT THIS IS A MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, MOVING PARTY FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1350(B). THEREFORE, THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO DATTANI. 2.AS TO AMERICAN WESTERN: EVEN THOUGH MOVING PARTY HAS SHOWN THAT DATTANI COULD HAVE WAIVED AMERICAN WESTERN RIGHTS PURSUANT TO THE CONTRACT BETWEEN FIRST REPUBLIC AND AMERICAN WESTERN, MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SHIFT ITS BURDEN AND SHOW THAT DATTANI IN FACT DID WAIVE EITHER FIRST REPUBLIC'S AND/OR AMERICAN WESTERN'S RIGHTS BASED ON THE LANGUAGE IN THE LEASE. Off Calendar Nov-20-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Lisa Roberts, Esq. (925-939-5330) of McNamara ,Ney, Beatty, Slattery, Borges and Ambacher LLP. for Defendant/Moving party MAVERICK RESTAURANT GROUP.
Marie Cheung-Truslow, Esq. (508-988-0340) of ISG Insurance for Plaintiff/Respondent American Western in Case #543635.

The Court announces the tentative ruling. ARGUED. Defendant contests the 2nd part of the tentative ruling on American Western, relying on the burden shifting on the Aguilar case.
Plaintiff responds there is nothing to show waiver of rights. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MAVERICK RESTAURANT GROUP, LLC's A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. 1.As to Dattani: In this consolidated action the claims by Dattani against the moving party include claims made in case 537353 and 543635. The Notice of Motion and the Separate Statement indicates that this motion is only made as to 543635, and therefore does not dispose of all the claims in this consolidated action. (See CCP 437c(a)). To the extent the moving party purports that this is a motion for summary adjudication, moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). Therefore, the motion for summary judgment/adjudication is denied as to Dattani. 2.As to American Western: Even though moving party has shown that Dattani could have waived American Western rights pursuant to the contract between First Republic and American Western, moving party failed to shift its burden and show that Dattani in fact did waive either First Republic's and/or American Western's rights based on the language in the Lease. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported (D501)
